blockingIssues.description: `getBranch()` catches every `GitCommandFailed` from `git symbolic-ref --short HEAD` and returns `HEAD`, masking real git failures as detached HEAD instead of surfacing an error.

`createTag()` assembles `git tag ${name} ${ref}` by raw string interpolation before handing it to the remote shell, so caller-controlled names/refs can break execution or execute unintended shell fragments.
`checkoutRef()` passes `git checkout ${ref}` straight through to the remote shell with no quoting or escaping, making the API unsafe for arbitrary branch/tag/SHA input.
The implementation exports `NetworkTimeout` instead of the feature's promised `TimeoutError`, so the public API does not match the contract callers are expected to use.
`AuthError` preserves raw `stderr` without any redaction path, so auth-failure output can leak credentials or sensitive repository URLs.
`checkSymlink()` converts SSH and command failures into `inactive`, causing `getSkillStatus()` and `listSkills()` to silently misreport transport or permission failures as legitimate inactive state.
`verifySync()` hard-codes GNU `sha256sum` locally and remotely, which is not portable to the documented macOS host and breaks checksum verification across the supported fleet.
`syncSkill()` rebuilds its own rsync SSH command and drops `HostConfig.keyPath`, so valid keyed host configurations can fail during the actual file transfer.
`activateSkill()` treats any non-broken symlink as already active without verifying it points at `repoPath/skillName`, so a stale or wrong target can remain in place.

`checkDrift()` computes ahead/behind counts on the drifted host using the reference host's SHA; if that SHA has not been fetched locally, Git errors and the implementation falls back to `diverged` with zero counts instead of reporting the required behind/ahead details.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers to mark `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` whenever they implement before creating/running red tests or skip required manual SSH verification, and require the specific deviation to be recorded in the handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Six milestone reviews (`git-ops-write-operations`, `git-ops-error-handling`, `skill-ops-discovery`, `skill-ops-sync`, `skill-ops-activation`, and `skill-ops-drift`) found transcript skeletons showing implementation before tests and/or missing manual verification while the handoffs still marked `followedProcedure: true`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether git/skill validation on `shuvtest` may mutate isolated remote repos for milestone and user-testing flows, or whether all remote validation must remain read-only.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `git-ops-write-operations` requires a manual pull on `shuvtest`, `skill-ops-drift` requires creating drift on a test host, but `.factory/library/user-testing.md` currently says remote SSH validation against `shuvtest` must stay read-only and must not modify remote hosts.

rejectedObservations.observation: Multiple reviews separately reported backend-worker TDD/verification adherence mismatches.
Document a repo-wide convention in shared guidance for reusing `packages/ssh/src/live.ts` transport options when invoking `rsync`/`scp` directly.
